food_subgroup: Fried or baked filled dumplings with cheese filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general wonton entry; no specific four-cheese wonton variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers filled pasta and dumpling products; no cheese-filled wonton-specific code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for cheese-filled wontons or wonton products as a food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of four cheese wontons consists of approximately 6 fried pieces weighing around 120 g, served with a hot honey dipping sauce on the side.

nutrient_source.title: Cheese Wontons, fried — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for cream cheese, cheddar, mozzarella, parmesan, and wheat wonton wrappers (fried)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods and SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for four-cheese wontons exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values estimated by weighted ingredient composition: wonton wrapper (wheat flour, fried) ~40% by weight, cream cheese ~25%, cheddar ~15%, mozzarella ~12%, parmesan ~8%. Values cross-referenced against USDA SR Legacy entries for each component. Energy calculated using Atwater factors. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Wontons, fried — analogous estimate based on GI of wheat-flour fried dumpling wrappers and cheese-filled pasta products; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for four-cheese wontons. GI estimated at 52 based on analogy with fried wheat-flour dumpling wrappers (GI ~50–55 per University of Sydney GI Database for similar wheat-based fried dough products) and the moderating effect of high fat and protein content from the cheese filling, which is well-established to lower postprandial glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 31.2) / 100 ≈ 16.2, where carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 26.0 × 1.2 = 31.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (480 mg per 100 g; approximately 576 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid four cheese wontons due to the high sodium load from multiple cheese varieties and the wonton wrapper; a single serving can contribute approximately 25% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by clinical guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~16 per serving) combined with high saturated fat from cheese filling
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume four cheese wontons in small portions; the moderate glycemic load of approximately 16 per serving and the high saturated fat content from the cheese blend can impair postprandial glucose control and insulin sensitivity if consumed in excess.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (9.8 g per 100 g), elevated sodium, and cholesterol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should treat four cheese wontons as an occasional indulgence only, as the combination of high saturated fat, sodium, and dietary cholesterol can adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels and blood pressure when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Four Cheese Wontons are a fusion snack dish consisting of crispy deep-fried wonton wrappers filled with a rich blend of four cheeses — typically cream cheese, cheddar, mozzarella, and parmesan. The dish is popular in Taiwanese-influenced casual dining and has been adopted by fusion restaurants in Singapore. The wonton wrappers are made from wheat flour dough, folded into triangular or purse shapes around the cheese filling, then deep-fried until golden and crispy. The exterior is light and crunchy while the interior is molten, creamy, and savoury. The dish is typically served with a hot honey dipping sauce that provides a sweet-spicy contrast to the rich cheese filling. Nutritionally, four cheese wontons are energy-dense at approximately 310 kcal per 100 g, with notable saturated fat (9.8 g/100 g) and sodium (480 mg/100 g) from the cheese blend. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low), but the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 16. The dish is not suitable for individuals on low-sodium or low-saturated-fat diets. It is vegetarian but not vegan, halal status depends on the cheese source, and it contains gluten and dairy allergens. Clinically, regular consumption is a concern for hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and Type 2 Diabetes management due to the combined sodium, saturated fat, and caloric density.
common_user_queries: How many calories in cheese wontons?
What cheese is in four cheese wontons?
Are cheese wontons suitable for diabetics?
What is the sodium content of cheese wontons?
What sauce goes with cheese wontons?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for four-cheese wontons exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA, ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values were estimated by weighted ingredient composition using USDA SR Legacy component data for cream cheese, cheddar, mozzarella, parmesan, and fried wheat wonton wrappers. GI was estimated by analogy with similar fried wheat-flour dumpling products from the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate for a composite estimate with no direct source match.
notes: 1. Food name normalised from 'Four Cheese Wantons' (input spelling) to 'Four Cheese Wontons' (standard English spelling); 'wanton' is a common Singaporean/Malaysian romanisation variant and is retained as an alias. 2. Food group reclassified from 'Dairy and eggs' (input) to 'Snacks' as the dish is a fried snack product; dairy is a key ingredient but does not define the food group at the dish level. 3. Cuisine tagged as 'Taiwanese-fusion' reflecting the wonton origin and the fusion cheese filling style common in Taiwanese casual dining exported to Singapore. 4. All nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data; no direct approved-source entry exists for this dish. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from component data and are not individually confirmed by a single direct source entry; these values carry medium confidence. 6. GI set to 52 (low) by analogy with fried wheat-flour dumpling products per University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for four-cheese wontons was found in any approved source. GL per serving = (52 × 31.2) / 100 ≈ 16.2 g, based on 120 g serving size. 7. Halal status marked false as parmesan and some cheddar varieties use animal rennet; halal certification would need to be confirmed per specific restaurant sourcing. 8. The input description 'Hot honey dipping sauce' has been incorporated as a serving accompaniment and ingredient synonym rather than a standalone food descriptor.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed. Identified that food group needed reclassification from 'Dairy and eggs' to 'Snacks'. Multilingual names added for Singaporean cuisine context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Nutrient values estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list — all entries confirmed present verbatim. Verified GI category threshold (52 ≤ 55 = low). Verified all ontology match_type values are from allowed set. Verified no zero core macros without justification. Verified all -1 sentinel values are documented in notes. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass.
